title: Topcoder Admin App - Consolidate Skills Manager
summary: This document outlines the architecture for consolidating the Skills Manager app into the system-admin app, including the creation of a new 'Platform' tab and the removal of role restrictions. The integration should maintain existing functionality without breaking current tests.

artifacts.design_doc_md:
# Architecture Document

## Requirements
- Consolidate Skills Manager into system-admin app.
- Create a 'Platform' tab.
- Remove role restrictions.

## Approach
- Integrate Skills Manager into the new tab.
- Ensure all functionalities are maintained.

## Rollout
- Deploy changes to production after thorough testing.

## Observability
- Monitor for any issues post-deployment.

## Acceptance Checklist
- [ ] New 'Platform' tab exists.
- [ ] Skills Manager loads correctly.
- [ ] No role restrictions are present.
- [ ] Existing tests pass.
tradeoffs: Pros: Simplifies admin management by consolidating tools.
Cons: Potential for increased complexity in the system-admin app.

assumptions: The 'Platform' tab does not currently exist in the system-admin app.
Existing tests for the Skills Manager app are functioning and will not be broken by this integration.
Admin credentials for testing will be provided.
plan: Review the current implementation of the Skills Manager app.
Create a new 'Platform' tab in the system-admin app if it does not exist.
Integrate the Skills Manager app into the new tab, ensuring it loads correctly.
Remove the rolesRequired feature to allow access for all administrators.
Clean up the configurations in production and development environments.
Test the integration to ensure existing functionalities remain intact.
Document the deployment and configuration changes in a README.
validations: Checked that all required sections are covered in the architecture document.
Ensured that the plan aligns with the requirements and constraints.

rubric_self_check.overall_notes: All required sections are addressed with clear actionable steps.
